working_on: Routine-protection refinement of S-0089's MCP-availability tightening. User clarification: MCP unavailability is easy to fix (Claude Desktop reboot); user just needs visibility. Routine sessions running overnight / AFK must NOT be killed by intermittent MCP. S-0089's `mempalace_diary_write_skipped_invalid_token` hard-fail blocks the close when substrate is alive but token is present — exactly the routine-killing scenario the user pushed back against. Convert the invalid-token path from hard-fail to soft-warn with mode-aware body (engine LOUD ⚠️ surface; routine standard one-line). Visibility still preserved via LOUD body for engine + standard for routine; routines close cleanly. Campaign plan reorder: Issue #41 (C3) shifts S-0090 → S-0091.
